inputs.label: Total Fixed Costs
inputs.businessContext: Sum of all fixed expenses (rent, salaries, insurance) per period.

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of all fixed expenses (rent, salaries, insurance) per period.

inputs.label: Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct material, labor, and overhead per unit.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct material, labor, and overhead per unit.

inputs.label: Actual Sales Volume
inputs.businessContext: Number of units actually sold in the period.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of units actually sold in the period.

inputs.label: Maximum Capacity Volume
inputs.businessContext: Theoretical maximum output under normal operating conditions.

businessContext_i18n.en: Theoretical maximum output under normal operating conditions.

inputs.label: Demand Uncertainty Factor
inputs.businessContext: Expected percentage variation in demand (coefficient of variation).

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected percentage variation in demand (coefficient of variation).

inputs.label: Cost Escalation Rate
inputs.businessContext: Annual expected increase in variable costs (inflation, supplier pricing).

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual expected increase in variable costs (inflation, supplier pricing).

inputs.label: Include Tax Effect
inputs.businessContext: If true, tax rate input is required and after-tax break-even is calculated.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, tax rate input is required and after-tax break-even is calculated.

inputs.label: Tax Rate
inputs.businessContext: Effective corporate tax rate, used only when includeTax is true.

businessContext_i18n.en: Effective corporate tax rate, used only when includeTax is true.

rules.message: Selling price must be greater than variable cost per unit to have a positive contribution margin.
Actual sales volume cannot be negative.
Capacity volume must be greater than zero.
Tax rate must be between 0% and 60% when tax effect is included.

thresholds.message: Safety margin is below 10% – high risk of falling below break-even.
Safety margin is critically low (<5%) – immediate action required.
Break-even point exceeds 90% of capacity – very low operating leverage.

formulas.label: Contribution Margin per Unit
formulas.description: Revenue left after variable costs to cover fixed costs and profit.

formulas.label: Contribution Margin Ratio
formulas.description: Fraction of each sales dollar available to cover fixed costs.

formulas.label: Break-Even Volume (Units)
formulas.description: Number of units that must be sold to cover all fixed and variable costs.

formulas.label: Break-Even Revenue
formulas.description: Revenue level at which total costs equal total revenue.

formulas.label: Safety Margin (%)
formulas.description: Percentage by which actual sales exceed break-even sales.

formulas.label: Adjusted Break-Even (with cost escalation & demand uncertainty)
formulas.description: Break-even volume adjusted for expected cost increases and demand variability.

primary.label: Safety Margin (%)
primary.description: Primary result: percentage of sales above break-even.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Fixed Cost Burden
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If fixed costs exceed 40% of total costs, risk increases.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Variable Cost Volatility
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High cost escalation rate (>5%) erodes margin quickly.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Capacity Underutilization
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If actual volume is less than 70% of capacity, fixed cost per unit rises.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Fixed Costs
suggestedActions.description: Negotiate rent, outsource non-core functions, or implement lean overhead reduction.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Selling Price
suggestedActions.description: Evaluate value-based pricing or add premium features to improve contribution margin.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Variable Cost Efficiency
suggestedActions.description: Apply Six Sigma DMAIC to reduce waste, renegotiate supplier contracts, or automate processes.

suggestedActions.label: Boost Sales Volume
suggestedActions.description: Launch marketing campaigns, expand distribution channels, or offer volume discounts.

suggestedActions.label: Hedge Cost Escalation
suggestedActions.description: Lock in long-term supplier contracts or use commodity hedging to stabilize variable costs.
